questions.prompt: Which word means 'yesterday'?
questions.explanation: Jučer = yesterday. Danas = today. Sutra = tomorrow. Preksutra = day after tomorrow.

questions.prompt: How is the past tense (perfekt) formed in Croatian?
questions.explanation: The perfekt = auxiliary (sam/si/je/smo/ste/su) + past participle (l-participle).

questions.prompt: Choose the correct past tense: 'Ja sam ___ u školi.' (raditi, masculine)
questions.explanation: For masculine singular: raditi → radio. Feminine would be radila.

questions.prompt: What does 'Jučer sam bio u kinu.' mean?
questions.correctAnswer: Yesterday I was at the cinema.
distractors: Yesterday I went to the cinema.
Yesterday I wanted to go to the cinema.
Tomorrow I will be at the cinema.
questions.explanation: Bio = was (masc past participle of biti). U kinu = at the cinema (locative).

questions.prompt: Which day is 'srijeda'?
questions.explanation: Ponedjeljak, utorak, srijeda, četvrtak, petak, subota, nedjelja.

questions.prompt: 'Ona je ___ pismo.' — Past tense of 'pisati' (feminine).
questions.explanation: Feminine singular past participle: pisati → pisala. Masculine would be pisao.

questions.prompt: What time expression means 'last week'?
questions.explanation: Prošli = last/past. Ovaj = this. Sljedeći = next. Svaki = every.

questions.prompt: Jučer sam ___ film. (gledati, masc singular past)
questions.explanation: Gledati → gledao (masculine singular past participle).

questions.prompt: Ona je ___ na tržnicu. (ići, fem singular past)
questions.explanation: Ići → išao (masc) / išla (fem). Irregular past participle.

questions.prompt: Mi smo ___ u restoranu. (jesti, plural past)
questions.explanation: Jesti → jeo (masc sg) / jela (fem sg) / jeli (plural).

questions.prompt: Oni su ___ hrvatsku pjesmu. (pjevati, plural past)
questions.explanation: Pjevati → pjevao/pjevala/pjevali. Plural past participle ends in -li.

questions.prompt: Što si ___ jučer? (raditi, masc singular past)
questions.explanation: Raditi → radio (masc sg past participle). Što si radio? = What did you do yesterday?

questions.prompt: U ___ sam bila na moru. (subota, locative = subotu for 'on Saturday')
questions.explanation: 'U subotu' = on Saturday. Days of the week use accusative after 'u' for 'on [day]'.

questions.prompt: Jučer sam kuhao ručak.
questions.explanation: Kuhao = cooked (masc past participle of kuhati). Ručak = lunch.

questions.prompt: Prošli tjedan smo putovali u Dubrovnik.
questions.correctAnswer: Last week we traveled to Dubrovnik.
acceptableAnswers: We traveled to Dubrovnik last week.
Last week we travelled to Dubrovnik.
questions.explanation: Prošli tjedan = last week. Putovali = traveled (plural past participle).

questions.prompt: Jesam li te probudio?
questions.explanation: Jesam li = did I (question form). Te = you (accusative). Probudio = woke up (masc).

questions.prompt: Ona je naučila hrvatski za dvije godine.
questions.correctAnswer: She learned Croatian in two years.
acceptableAnswers: She learnt Croatian in two years.
questions.explanation: Naučila = learned (fem past participle of naučiti). Za dvije godine = in two years.

questions.prompt: U petak smo izašli van.
questions.explanation: U petak = on Friday. Izašli = went out (plural past of izaći). Van = out.

questions.prompt: I ate breakfast at eight o'clock.
acceptableAnswers: Ja sam doručkovao u osam sati.
questions.hint: Croatian uses 'sati' (hours) after the number for telling time.
questions.explanation: Doručkovao = ate breakfast (masc past). U osam sati = at 8 o'clock.

questions.prompt: We watched a movie yesterday.
questions.explanation: Gledali = watched (plural past). Film = movie.

questions.prompt: She went to the store on Monday.
questions.correctAnswer: Išla je u trgovinu u ponedjeljak.
acceptableAnswers: U ponedjeljak je išla u trgovinu.
Ona je išla u trgovinu u ponedjeljak.
questions.explanation: Išla = went (fem past of ići). Trgovina = store. U ponedjeljak = on Monday.

questions.prompt: What did you do on the weekend? (informal)
questions.explanation: Što si radio = what did you do (informal, masc). Za vikend = on/for the weekend.

questions.prompt: They arrived on Thursday evening.
questions.explanation: Stigli = arrived (plural past of stići). Navečer = in the evening.

questions.prompt: Give the past tense (perfekt) of 'raditi' for all persons (masculine singular forms, plural -li).
questions.correctAnswer: ja sam radio, ti si radio, on/ona/ono je radio, mi smo radili, vi ste radili, oni/one/ona su radili
questions.explanation: The perfekt combines the present of 'biti' with the l-participle: radio (m), radila (f), radili (pl).
questions.exampleSentence: Cijeli dan sam radio, a navečer sam se odmarao.
questions.exampleTranslation: I worked all day, and in the evening I rested.

questions.prompt: Give the past tense (perfekt) of 'ići' for all persons (masculine forms).
questions.correctAnswer: ja sam išao, ti si išao, on/ona/ono je išao, mi smo išli, vi ste išli, oni/one/ona su išli
questions.hint: Ići has an irregular past participle: išao/išla/išli.
questions.explanation: Ići → išao (masc sg), išla (fem sg), išli (plural). The participle is completely different from the infinitive.
questions.exampleSentence: Išli smo na more prošlog ljeta.
questions.exampleTranslation: We went to the seaside last summer.

questions.prompt: Give the past tense (perfekt) of 'jesti' for all persons (masculine forms).
questions.correctAnswer: ja sam jeo, ti si jeo, on/ona/ono je jeo, mi smo jeli, vi ste jeli, oni/one/ona su jeli
questions.hint: The past participle of jesti is irregular: jeo/jela/jeli.
questions.explanation: Jesti → jeo (masc sg), jela (fem sg), jeli (plural).

questions.prompt: Give the past tense (perfekt) of 'biti' for all persons (masculine forms).
questions.correctAnswer: ja sam bio, ti si bio, on/ona/ono je bio, mi smo bili, vi ste bili, oni/one/ona su bili
questions.hint: Even 'biti' has a past participle: bio/bila/bili.
questions.explanation: Biti → bio (masc), bila (fem), bili (plural). 'Sam bio' = I was.
questions.exampleSentence: Bili smo u Splitu prošle godine.
questions.exampleTranslation: We were in Split last year.

questions.prompt: Give the past tense (perfekt) of 'kupiti' for all persons (masculine forms).
questions.correctAnswer: ja sam kupio, ti si kupio, on/ona/ono je kupio, mi smo kupili, vi ste kupili, oni/one/ona su kupili
questions.explanation: Kupiti → kupio (masc), kupila (fem), kupili (pl). Regular -iti past participle pattern.
questions.exampleTranslation: I bought a new phone yesterday.
